axiom obj : Type
axiom Mary : obj

axiom Homeowner : obj → Prop
axiom LearnedBotany : obj → Prop
axiom UnderstoodNeeds : obj → Prop
axiom ThrivingPlants : obj → Prop
axiom SoughtAdvice : obj → Prop

axiom Mary_is_homeowner : Homeowner Mary
axiom Learn_implies_understand : ∀ x : obj, Homeowner x → (LearnedBotany x → UnderstoodNeeds x)
axiom Understand_implies_thrive : ∀ x : obj, Homeowner x → (UnderstoodNeeds x → ThrivingPlants x)
axiom Learn_or_advice : ∀ x : obj, Homeowner x → (LearnedBotany x ∨ SoughtAdvice x)
axiom Mary_not_thrive : ¬ ThrivingPlants Mary

theorem mary_sought_advice : SoughtAdvice Mary :=
by
  have not_understand : ¬ UnderstoodNeeds Mary :=
    by
      intro U
      apply Mary_not_thrive
      apply Understand_implies_thrive Mary Mary_is_homeowner
      exact U
  have not_learn : ¬ LearnedBotany Mary :=
    by
      intro L
      apply not_understand
      apply Learn_implies_understand Mary Mary_is_homeowner
      exact L
  have hdisj : LearnedBotany Mary ∨ SoughtAdvice Mary := Learn_or_advice Mary Mary_is_homeowner
  cases hdisj with
  | inl L =>
      exfalso
      exact not_learn L
  | inr S =>
      exact S
</lean>

  exact H_flooding Dallas ho

<lean>
axiom Party : Type
axiom SamParty : Party

axiom BirthdayParty : Party → Prop
axiom Cake : Party → Prop
axiom IceCream : Party → Prop
axiom BroughtDish : Party → Prop
axiom EnoughFood : Party → Prop

-- If someone brought a dish to share at a party, then there will be enough food for everyone.
axiom R1 : ∀ p : Party, BroughtDish p → EnoughFood p
-- If it is a birthday party, there will be cake and ice cream.
axiom R2 : ∀ p : Party, BirthdayParty p → (Cake p ∧ IceCream p)
-- Either someone brought a dish to share, or there was no birthday cake.
axiom R3 : ∀ p : Party, (BroughtDish p) ∨ (¬ Cake p)

-- It is Sam's birthday party.
axiom B1 : BirthdayParty SamParty

theorem sam_enough_food : EnoughFood SamParty := by
  have hcake : Cake SamParty := by
    have h := R2 SamParty B1
    exact And.left h
  have hdisj := R3 SamParty
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hbrought =>
      exact R1 SamParty hbrought
  | inr hnoCake =>
      have f : False := hnoCake hcake
      exact False.elim f
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

    intro h_prac
    exact h_not_fast (h_prac_to_fast h_prac)
  -- Use the disjunction: Practice Sarah ∨ Listen Sarah
  cases Disj with
  | inl h_prac =>
      exact False.elim (h_not_prac h_prac)
  | inr h_listen =>
      exact h_listen

axiom Practice : obj → Prop           -- practiced guitar (regularly / scales & chords daily)
axiom Listen : obj → Prop             -- listens attentively to music
axiom Technique : obj → Prop          -- technique improved
axiom LearnFast : obj → Prop          -- able to learn songs faster

-- Rules learned
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Practice x → Technique x
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Technique x → LearnFast x

-- Either practiced or listened (or both)
axiom Disj : Practice Sarah ∨ Listen Sarah

-- Goal: From "Sarah is not able to learn songs faster", show she listens attentively to music.
theorem sarah_listens : (¬ LearnFast Sarah) → Listen Sarah := by
  intro h_not_fast
  -- From practicing, technique improves, hence learns faster
  have h_prac_to_fast : Practice Sarah → LearnFast Sarah := by
    intro h_prac
    have h_tech : Technique Sarah := R1 Sarah h_prac
    exact R2 Sarah h_tech
  -- Therefore, if she cannot learn faster, she did not practice
  have h_not_prac : ¬ Practice Sarah := by
    intro h_prac
    exact h_not_fast (h_prac_to_fast h_prac)
  -- Use the disjunction: Practice Sarah ∨ Listen Sarah
  cases Disj with
  | inl h_prac =>
      exact False.elim (h_not_prac h_prac)
  | inr h_listen =>
      exact h_listen

axiom OfferGreatService : Prop
axiom HostAuthorEvents : Prop
axiom MoreCustomersVisit : Prop
axiom RevenueImproves : Prop
axiom ReputationGrows : Prop

-- If they offer great customer service, their reputation will grow.
axiom great_service_implies_reputation : OfferGreatService → ReputationGrows
-- If they host author events, more customers will visit.
axiom events_imply_customers : HostAuthorEvents → MoreCustomersVisit
-- The owner either provides excellent service or hosts events.
axiom service_or_events : OfferGreatService ∨ HostAuthorEvents
-- If customer traffic increases, revenue will improve.
axiom customers_imply_revenue : MoreCustomersVisit → RevenueImproves

-- Given the service quality decreased (i.e., not OfferGreatService), revenue improves.
theorem revenue_improves_from_decreased_service (hdec : ¬ OfferGreatService) : RevenueImproves := by
  have hEvents : HostAuthorEvents :=
    Or.elim service_or_events
      (fun hServ : OfferGreatService => False.elim (hdec hServ))
      (fun hEv : HostAuthorEvents => hEv)
  have hCust : MoreCustomersVisit := events_imply_customers hEvents
  exact customers_imply_revenue hCust
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Mary : obj

axiom SnowOnGround : Prop
axiom BelowFreezing : Prop
axiom EnoughSnow : Prop
axiom BuildsSnowman : obj → Prop
axiom LovesWinter : obj → Prop

-- If the temperature dropped below freezing, then there would be snow on the ground.
axiom BF_implies_Snow : BelowFreezing → SnowOnGround

-- If someone builds a snowman, then there is enough snow.
axiom Build_implies_Enough : ∀ x : obj, BuildsSnowman x → EnoughSnow

-- If someone loves winter, then they build snowmen.
axiom Love_implies_Build : ∀ x : obj, LovesWinter x → BuildsSnowman x

-- Either it was below freezing, or there was not enough snow, or it was below freezing and there was not enough snow.
axiom Certainty : BelowFreezing ∨ ¬ EnoughSnow ∨ (BelowFreezing ∧ ¬ EnoughSnow)

-- From no snow on the ground, we can derive that Mary does not love winter.
theorem no_snow_implies_not_love_mary : ¬ SnowOnGround → ¬ LovesWinter Mary := by
  intro hNoSnow
  -- First, derive not below freezing.
  have hNotBF : ¬ BelowFreezing := by
    intro hBF
    apply hNoSnow
    exact BF_implies_Snow hBF
  -- From the disjunction, derive not enough snow.
  have hNotEN : ¬ EnoughSnow := by
    cases Certainty with
    | inl hBF =>
      intro _
      exact False.elim (hNotBF hBF)
    | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hNotEN => exact hNotEN
      | inr hBoth => exact hBoth.right
  -- If Mary loved winter, she'd build a snowman, implying enough snow, contradicting not enough snow.
  intro hLove
  have hBuild : BuildsSnowman Mary := Love_implies_Build Mary hLove
  have hEN : EnoughSnow := Build_implies_Enough Mary hBuild
  exact hNotEN hEN
</lean>

<lean>
axiom Person : Type
axiom John : Person

axiom CloseLunch : Prop
axiom HungryCoworkers : Person → Prop
axiom PreparedWell : Person → Prop
axiom Ready : Person → Prop
axiom Diligent : Person → Prop

-- If it is close to lunchtime, then that person's coworkers are hungry.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, CloseLunch → HungryCoworkers x
-- If a person prepared well, then they are ready.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Person, PreparedWell x → Ready x
-- Either it is close to lunchtime, or John is not ready, or both close to lunchtime and John not ready.
axiom A3 : CloseLunch ∨ ¬Ready John ∨ (CloseLunch ∧ ¬Ready John)
-- Everyone: if someone is diligent, they prepare well.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Person, Diligent x → PreparedWell x

-- From "John's coworkers are not hungry" we get "it is not close to lunchtime".
theorem not_close_of_not_hungry : ¬HungryCoworkers John → ¬CloseLunch := by
  intro nh
  intro cl
  have h : HungryCoworkers John := A1 John cl
  exact nh h

-- From "John's coworkers are not hungry" we get "John is not ready".
theorem not_ready_of_not_hungry : ¬HungryCoworkers John → ¬Ready John := by
  intro nh
  have ncl : ¬CloseLunch := not_close_of_not_hungry nh
  have disj := A3
  cases disj with
  | inl cl =>
      exact False.elim (ncl cl)
  | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl nready => exact nready
      | inr clnready =>
          exact clnready.right

-- Hence, from "John's coworkers are not hungry" we conclude "John is not diligent".
theorem not_diligent_of_not_hungry : ¬HungryCoworkers John → ¬Diligent John := by
  intro nh
  have nr : ¬Ready John := not_ready_of_not_hungry nh
  intro dj
  have prep : PreparedWell John := A4 John dj
  have r : Ready John := A2 John prep
  exact nr r
</lean>

ANSWER: Failure

-- If someone volunteers at a local shelter, they will help the community.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Volunteers x → Helps x
-- If someone adopts a pet from the shelter, then they provide a home to a pet in need.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, Adopts x → ProvidesHome x
-- Either someone volunteers at the shelter, or they do not provide a home to a pet in need,
-- or someone volunteered at the shelter, and they did not provide a home to a pet in need.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Volunteers x ∨ ¬ProvidesHome x ∨ (Volunteers x ∧ ¬ProvidesHome x)
-- If someone helps the community, then they feel a sense of fulfillment.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, Helps x → FeelsFulfillment x

theorem casey_fulfillment_from_adoption : Adopts Casey → FeelsFulfillment Casey := by
  intro hAd
  have hPH : ProvidesHome Casey := R2 Casey hAd
  have hVol : Volunteers Casey := by
    have disj := R3 Casey
    cases disj with
    | inl hv => exact hv
    | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl nph =>
        have : False := nph hPH
        exact False.elim this
      | inr hvnph =>
        exact hvnph.left
  have hHelps : Helps Casey := R1 Casey hVol
  exact R4 Casey hHelps

axiom CanPickUpMaterials : Person → Prop
axiom LearnNewSkills : Person → Prop

-- If the special order is ready, they can pick up the materials they need.
axiom A2 : ∀ p : Person, SpecialOrderReady → CanPickUpMaterials p
-- Either the package will arrive on time or the special order will be ready.
axiom A3 : OnTimePackage ∨ SpecialOrderReady
-- If someone starts his new project, they will learn some new skills.
axiom A4 : ∀ p : Person, StartNewProject p → LearnNewSkills p

-- Max was unable to pick up the materials he needed.
axiom Max_could_not_pickup : ¬ CanPickUpMaterials Max

  -- From Max not being able to pick up materials, the special order was not ready
  have not_special : ¬ SpecialOrderReady := by

    exact Max_could_not_pickup hPick
  -- From the disjunction and not_special, the package arrived on time
  have on_time : OnTimePackage := by
    cases A3 with
    | inl h => exact h
    | inr h => exact False.elim (not_special h)

  have started : StartNewProject Max := A1 Max on_time
  -- Hence Max learns new skills
  exact A4 Max started

axiom Wet : Prop
axiom Washed : Prop
axiom UsedWater : Prop
axiom Uncoiled : Prop
axiom Soapy : Prop

-- Premises
axiom P1 : Wet → Rain
axiom P2 : Washed → UsedWater
axiom P3 : Wet ∨ ¬UsedWater ∨ (Wet ∧ ¬UsedWater)
axiom P4 : Uncoiled → Washed
axiom P5 : Uncoiled ∨ Soapy

theorem sponge_soapy_of_no_rain : ¬Rain → Soapy :=
  fun hNotRain =>
    -- From Wet → Rain and ¬Rain, get ¬Wet
    let hNotWet : ¬Wet := fun hWet => hNotRain (P1 hWet)
    -- From the trilemma and ¬Wet, deduce ¬UsedWater
    let hNotUsed : ¬UsedWater :=
      by
        cases P3 with
        | inl hWet =>
            exact False.elim (hNotWet hWet)
        | inr h2 =>
            cases h2 with
            | inl hNotU => exact hNotU
            | inr hWetAndNotU => exact hWetAndNotU.right
    -- From Uncoiled ∨ Soapy, conclude Soapy (else contradiction via UsedWater)
    show Soapy from
      (match P5 with
       | Or.inl hUncoiled =>
           let hUsed : UsedWater := P2 (P4 hUncoiled)
           False.elim (hNotUsed hUsed)
       | Or.inr hSoapy => hSoapy)
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Jesse : obj

axiom Sprinting : obj → Prop
axiom Stretching : obj → Prop
axiom RunningImproved : obj → Prop
axiom Flexible : obj → Prop
axiom LessInjury : obj → Prop

-- If someone did sprint workouts frequently, their running form and stamina improved.
axiom Sprint_improves_run : ∀ x : obj, Sprinting x → RunningImproved x
-- Either they were sprinting frequently, or they were taking stretching classes (or both).
axiom Sprint_or_Stretch : ∀ x : obj, Sprinting x ∨ Stretching x
-- If someone takes stretching classes, they become more flexible and less prone to injury.
axiom Stretch_gives_benefits : ∀ x : obj, Stretching x → (Flexible x ∧ LessInjury x)

-- Given: Improving running form and stamina did not happen for Jesse.
axiom not_run_improved_Jesse : ¬ RunningImproved Jesse

theorem jesse_flexible_and_less_injury : Flexible Jesse ∧ LessInjury Jesse := by
  -- First, Jesse was not sprinting frequently (by contrapositive reasoning)
  have not_sprinting : ¬ Sprinting Jesse := by
    intro hs
    have ri : RunningImproved Jesse := Sprint_improves_run Jesse hs
    exact not_run_improved_Jesse ri
  -- From the disjunction and not_sprinting, conclude Jesse took stretching classes
  have stretch_Jesse : Stretching Jesse := by
    have h := Sprint_or_Stretch Jesse
    cases h with
    | inl hs =>
        have f : False := not_sprinting hs
        exact False.elim f
    | inr ht => exact ht
  -- Therefore, Jesse became more flexible and less prone to injury
  exact Stretch_gives_benefits Jesse stretch_Jesse
</lean>

axiom VocabImproved : Person → Prop
axiom RealConversations : Person → Prop
axiom GainListeningComp : Person → Prop

-- If someone speaks daily with native speakers, their fluency and vocabulary improve.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, SpeaksDaily x → (FluencyImproved x ∧ VocabImproved x)
-- If someone's fluency improves, they can have real conversations.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Person, FluencyImproved x → RealConversations x
-- Either someone speaks daily with native speakers or they listen to educational podcasts (or both).
axiom A3 : ∀ x : Person, SpeaksDaily x ∨ ListensPod x
-- If someone listens to educational podcasts, they gain valuable listening comprehension.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Person, ListensPod x → GainListeningComp x

theorem emma_listening_from_no_convo : (¬ RealConversations Emma) → GainListeningComp Emma := by
  intro hNotR
  have s_or_l : SpeaksDaily Emma ∨ ListensPod Emma := A3 Emma
  have hNotS : ¬ SpeaksDaily Emma := by
    intro hS
    have hpair := A1 Emma hS
    have hf : FluencyImproved Emma := And.left hpair
    have r : RealConversations Emma := A2 Emma hf
    exact hNotR r
  cases s_or_l with
  | inl hS =>
      have contra : False := hNotS hS
      exact False.elim contra
  | inr hL =>
      exact A4 Emma hL
</lean>

ANSWER: Yes

axiom John : obj

axiom Sale : Prop
axiom BeginMonth : Prop
axiom BuyMore : obj → Prop
axiom GoShop : obj → Prop
axiom GetAll : obj → Prop
axiom SoldOut : obj → Prop

-- If the store has a sale, then people will buy more items.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Sale → BuyMore x
-- If someone goes grocery shopping, then they will get everything on their list.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, GoShop x → GetAll x
-- Either the store has a sale, or they did not get everything on their list,
-- or there was a sale and they still did not get all their groceries (for John).
axiom A3 : Sale ∨ (¬ GetAll John ∨ (Sale ∧ ¬ GetAll John))
-- If it is the beginning of the month, then people go grocery shopping.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, BeginMonth → GoShop x
-- Either it is the beginning of the month, or John's favorite cereal is sold out,
-- or both are true.
axiom A5 : BeginMonth ∨ (SoldOut John ∨ (BeginMonth ∧ SoldOut John))

theorem soldout_of_not_buymore : ¬ BuyMore John → SoldOut John := by
  intro Hnb
  -- From ¬(BuyMore John) and (Sale → BuyMore John), deduce ¬Sale
  have h_not_sale : ¬ Sale := fun hs => Hnb ((A1 John) hs)
  -- From the disjunction A3 and ¬Sale, deduce ¬GetAll John
  have h_not_getall : ¬ GetAll John := by
    cases A3 with
    | inl hs =>
        exact False.elim (h_not_sale hs)
    | inr rest =>
        cases rest with
        | inl hneg => exact hneg
        | inr hboth => exact hboth.right
  -- From ¬GetAll John and (GoShop → GetAll), deduce ¬GoShop John
  have h_not_goshop : ¬ GoShop John := fun hg => h_not_getall ((A2 John) hg)
  -- From ¬GoShop John and (BeginMonth → GoShop), deduce ¬BeginMonth
  have h_not_begin : ¬ BeginMonth := fun hb => h_not_goshop ((A4 John) hb)
  -- From A5 and ¬BeginMonth, deduce SoldOut John
  cases A5 with
  | inl hb =>
      exact False.elim (h_not_begin hb)
  | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl hsold => exact hsold
      | inr hboth => exact hboth.right
</lean>

axiom Person : Type
axiom Sam : Person

axiom Hungry : Person → Prop
axiom MakesPancakes : Person → Prop
axiom UsesStove : Person → Prop
axiom AteDinnerLastNight : Person → Prop

axiom StoveOn : Prop
axiom KitchenHot : Prop

-- If the stove is on, the kitchen gets hot.
axiom H1 : StoveOn → KitchenHot
-- If someone makes pancakes, they use the stove.
axiom H2 : ∀ x : Person, MakesPancakes x → UsesStove x
-- Either the stove was on, or no one used the stove.
axiom H3 : StoveOn ∨ ∀ x : Person, ¬UsesStove x
-- If someone is hungry, they make pancakes.
axiom H4 : ∀ x : Person, Hungry x → MakesPancakes x
-- If Sam did not eat dinner last night, he was hungry this morning.
axiom H5 : ¬AteDinnerLastNight Sam → Hungry Sam
-- The kitchen did not get hot.
axiom H6 : ¬KitchenHot

theorem Sam_ate_dinner_last_night : AteDinnerLastNight Sam := by
  classical
  by_contra hNot
  have hHungry : Hungry Sam := H5 hNot
  have hPanc : MakesPancakes Sam := H4 Sam hHungry
  have hUse : UsesStove Sam := H2 Sam hPanc
  have hNoUseAll : ∀ x : Person, ¬UsesStove x := by
    cases H3 with
    | inl hOn =>
      -- From StoveOn we get KitchenHot, contradicting H6; from False we can derive any ¬UsesStove x.
      exact fun x => fun _ => False.elim (H6 (H1 hOn))
    | inr h =>
      exact h
  have hNoUseSam : ¬UsesStove Sam := hNoUseAll Sam
  exact hNoUseSam hUse
</lean>

  intro hRain

  have hRelax : Relaxed x := (hot_implies_relax x) hHot
  have hDisj : WorkedOutHard x ∨ ¬ Relaxed x := hard_or_not_relax x
  cases hDisj with
  | inl hHard =>
    exact (hard_implies_sore x) hHard
